channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Success Lab: Mindfulness
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Come learn what all the buzz is about and why companies like Google, Twitter, PayPal, LinkedIn and many more in Silicon Valley are employing mindfulness techniques. We’ll discuss the benefits, particularly in assisting with stress, overall cognition, and how it can help you progress towards your goals.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Lab: Academic Prep for Finals
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Learn how to finish the semester strong through preparation and savvy techniques for your final exams.
item.author: Students

item.title: Register for Commencement!
item.description: In the Tassel platform, register for commencement. Confirm attendance, approve your name recording, and claim tickets. Registration closes on Sunday, April 19th, 2026. If you have any questions, reach out to commencement@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: The RPI Experience: What’s New, What’s Next Virtual Session
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Curious what’s changing with The RPI Experience (Formerly The Arch) and what it means for you? Come join us for a second live virtual chat where we’ll break down the latest updates, walk through expectations, and help you plan your next steps with confidence. We will also cover key details about Discovery semester registration for summer, fall, and spring, and answer your questions so you feel prepared for what’s ahead!
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week of 4/5-4/11
item.description: Week of 4/5- 4/11: Need some last minute review for your upcoming exam? Stop by for a Puck Cookie and a Review Session with our Success Center Tutors! -Strengths: Monday, 4/6 (5-7pm) at the Success Center (Beta Classroom) -MAU: Monday, 4/6 (5-7pm) at the Success Center
item.author: Students

item.title: Easter Holiday Notice: Sunday, April 5th
item.description: No drop-in tutoring during the Easter holiday at the Success Center. Small group & one-to-one sessions may still be available to book.
item.author: Students

item.title: FE Exam Info Session
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 7:00 PM - 8:00 PM - Chi Epsilon will host a FE Exam Info Session. They will provide information on how to sign up and where to find the tools to help you pass. You won’t want to miss out on having these tips and tricks.
item.author: Students

item.title: Meet Morgan Stanley Technology (Virtual Information Session)
item.description: This session is for RPI students interested in pursuing a summer internship or full-time opportunity in financial services technology. Students will meet with business representatives from the firm to gain valuable career advice and mentorship. Register on Handshake here: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1920821/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Colloquia - Cameron Musco
item.description: Date: Monday, April 6, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Cameron Musco Associate Professor at University of Massachusetts Amherst Operator Learning Through the Lens of Linear Algebra In this talk, I will discuss a research program that seeks to understand the sample complexity of operator learning, which is a critical bottleneck in many applications.
item.author: Students

item.title: Entrepreneur Speaker Series - “Degrees of Freedom”
item.description: Date: Monday, April 6, 2026 6:30 PM - 8:00 PM - The RPI Alumni Chapter of NJ invites current students to a Zoom panel discussion with 3 successful alumni entrepreneurs: Larry Furino ’03, Joe Evangelista ’10, and Shruti Gupta ’22. The speakers will offer practical guidance on career development, leadership, and adapting in an evolving professional landscape.
item.author: Students

item.title: RPI Community Easter Dinner
item.description: Date: Sunday, April 5, 2026 6:30 PM - 9:30 PM - This Dinner is a yearly event hosted by Rensselaer Christian Association, Cru RPI, and Rensselaer Newman to serve the RPI community. We invite all of you to come enjoy a good meal and fellowship. If you are planning to attend, we ask that you RSVP using the form below. This will allow us to make sure we have enough food for everyone. https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc-4w8TCGuyrUUZrIVWBXnG0Ws7ScX8fNpxbIryK5BSxkr5qw/viewform
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar Friday April 10: RPI Digital Asset Management (DAM) System
item.description: Join Jim Evans, Director of MultiMedia Services, on Friday April 10 from 11am to 12pm, for a live webinar introducing Rensselaer’s Digital Asset Management System. Discover how to access campus‑wide photography, multimedia files, and institutional assets—all in one place. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: RARE High School Student Advances to International Science and Engineering Fair
item.description: The Rensselaer Astrobiology Research and Education (RARE) Center congratulates a local high school student, Mikaila, on being named a finalist for the Regeneron International Science and Engineering Fair (ISEF) in May 2026. After earning first place at the Westchester Science & Engineering Fair and recognition from the Association for Women Geoscientists, she will present research conducted over two summers at the RARE Center.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Sciences Department - Class of ’27 Lecture Series | April 7 & April 8
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 7, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - The Class of ’27 Lecture Series, established in 1960 to honor the first chair of the Math Sciences Department, holds two special lectures annually. Lecture I, on April 7, will present “Nonlocal PDEs and Quantum Optics”. Lecture II, on April 8, features “Inverse Problems in Quantum Optics”, by speaker John C. Schotland from Yale University.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Sciences Department - Class of ’27 Lecture Series | April 7 & April 8
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 8, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - The Class of ’27 Lecture Series, established in 1960 to honor the first chair of the Math Sciences Department, holds two special lectures annually. Lecture I, on April 7, will present “Nonlocal PDEs and Quantum Optics”. Lecture II, on April 8, features “Inverse Problems in Quantum Optics”, by speaker John C. Schotland from Yale University.
item.author: Community

item.title: Biological Sciences Seminar - Dr. Rakez Kayed
item.description: Date: Monday, April 6, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Dr. Rakez Kayed, Professor of Neurology, Neurobiology, Cell Biology and Anatomy at University of Texas Medical Branch, Galveston TX will present a seminar titled “Disease-Specific and Alzheimer’s APOE Isoform-Specific Polymorphism of Tau Aggregates” on Monday, April 6 2026. Seminar will be from 12:00-1:00pm in the CBIS Auditorium.
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - How to Recognize and Report Business Email Compromise (BEC) Emails
item.description: Business Email Compromise (BEC) is a sophisticated cybercrime targeting Organizations and Universities where attackers impersonate trusted individuals including Executives, Faculty or Staff, and trusted vendors. Through impersonation, these bad actors will attempt to trick victims into transferring funds, revealing sensitive data, and other social engineering tactics attempting to extract personally identifiable information (PII).
item.author: Community

item.title: 2026 Faculty and Staff Climate Survey Launched
item.description:
Date: Friday, April 3, 2026 (all day) - The 2026 Faculty and Staff Climate Survey launched by email on April 2, 2026. Your honest and candid opinions about the environment at RPI and how it affects your ability to do you work and thrive, professionally and personally, are important. You will find a personal link to complete the survey in your email from President Schmidt. Please submit the completely anonymous survey by April 30, 2026. If you have questions, please email OIRA at oira@rpi.edu .

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
